skill.name: stitch-design-taste
skill.description: Semantic Design System Skill for Google Stitch. Generates agent-friendly DESIGN.md files that enforce premium, anti-generic UI standards — strict typography, calibrated color, asymmetric layouts, perpetual micro-motion, and hardware-accelerated performance.
skill.summary: Generate premium, anti-generic UI design specifications for Google Stitch with this semantic design system skill.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 205 potential issues, all evaluated as false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation files describing design system specifications. No executable code, shell commands, or cryptographic operations exist. All detected patterns are design terminology being misinterpreted by the scanner (e.g., CSS hash notation, font weight values, design system references). Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Generate Premium UI Design Systems for Google Stitch
content.value_statement: Claude agents often produce generic, AI-looking interfaces. This skill enforces strict design standards to generate premium, anti-generic UI specifications that avoid common pitfalls like Inter font, neon glows, and centered hero sections.
actual_capabilities: Generate DESIGN.md files with calibrated color palettes, strict typography rules, and explicit anti-patterns
Enforce premium design standards that avoid common AI design clichés (Inter font, purple gradients, neon glows)
Provide responsive design specifications that work across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
Define motion philosophy with spring physics parameters and perpetual micro-interactions
Create asymmetric layouts with Bento grid architectures instead of generic 3-column cards
Encode comprehensive banned patterns list to ensure high-quality, non-generic output
limitations: Outputs markdown documentation only — does not generate actual code or UI components
Does not execute shell commands or access filesystem at runtime — purely descriptive
Requires Google Stitch access to apply the generated design specifications
Design choices are subjective — the enforced aesthetic may not fit all project types

use_cases.title: Create Premium Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Generate design specifications for high-end landing pages that avoid generic AI aesthetics. Perfect for startups wanting professional, distinctive web presence.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers and designers working on client projects

use_cases.title: Build Consistent Design Systems
use_cases.description: Establish consistent visual language across a product suite by documenting typography, color, spacing, and component behaviors in a single source of truth.
use_cases.target_user: Product teams standardizing design across multiple features

use_cases.title: Prototype with Design Constraints
use_cases.description: Use the anti-patterns list as guardrails when rapidly prototyping. The explicit bans prevent common design mistakes before they happen.
use_cases.target_user: UI/UX designers seeking to enforce premium quality standards

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Design Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stitch-design-taste skill to generate a DESIGN.md for a [describe your project type, e.g., 'SaaS dashboard'] with a [creativity level 1-10] creativity setting and [density level 1-10] density.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use case - specify project type and style preferences

prompt_templates.title: Design for Specific Vibe
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stitch-design-taste skill to create a DESIGN.md for [describe your project]. The vibe is [describe vibe, e.g., 'editorial magazine with bold typography']. Set variance to 8 and motion to 6.
prompt_templates.scenario: Specify project with desired aesthetic and motion characteristics

prompt_templates.title: Dense Data Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stitch-design-taste skill to generate a DESIGN.md for a data-heavy dashboard. Set density to 9, variance to 7, and enable monospace for all numerical values. Include perpetual animations for status indicators.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex use case - high-density data visualization with specific technical requirements

prompt_templates.title: Minimal Portfolio Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stitch-design-taste skill to create a DESIGN.md for a minimalist portfolio. Set creativity to 3, density to 2, variance to 2. Use Electric Blue as the single accent. No serif fonts.

output_examples.input: Generate a design spec for a fintech dashboard
output_examples.output: A DESIGN.md file with: Canvas White (#F9FAFB) background, Charcoal Ink (#18181B) text, Emerald Signal (#10B981) accent for growth metrics, Geist font at weight 700-900 for headlines, monospace for all numbers, spring physics animation specs (stiffness: 100, damping: 20), and explicit bans on Inter font, neon glows, and generic placeholder data.

output_examples.input: Create a design spec for an editorial magazine site
output_examples.output: A DESIGN.md file with: High creativity (9), high variance (8), asymmetric hero layouts with inline image typography, distinctive modern serifs (Fraunces, Instrument Serif) for display, strict 65ch max-width for body text, and motion orchestration with staggered reveals.

best_practices: Start with the atmosphere description — understand the vibe before detailing tokens to ensure cohesive design language
Pick exactly one accent color and stick to it throughout the specification — multiple accents dilute premium feel
Include explicit anti-patterns — the banned list is what makes the output non-generic and distinctive
anti_patterns: Using Inter font — it creates generic AI-looking interfaces; use Geist, Satoshi, Cabinet Grotesk, or Outfit instead
Adding purple or neon gradient effects — this screams 'AI-generated' and is strictly banned in premium contexts
Centered hero layouts — these are generic and banned; use asymmetric split-screen or left-aligned structures instead

faq.question: What is Google Stitch?
faq.answer: Google Stitch is a screen generation tool that uses AI to create UI designs from natural language descriptions. This skill generates DESIGN.md files that serve as the semantic specification layer for Stitch.

faq.question: Does this skill generate actual code?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates markdown documentation (DESIGN.md) containing design specifications. The output is meant to be read by Stitch or other AI agents to inform their design generation.

faq.question: Why are there so many banned patterns?
faq.answer: The anti-patterns list is the core value of this skill. Common AI design clichés (Inter font, neon glows, centered heroes) produce generic-looking interfaces. The bans enforce a curated, premium aesthetic.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-Stitch projects?
faq.answer: Yes. While designed for Google Stitch, the generated DESIGN.md files can be used by any AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Claude Code) to produce consistently premium UI implementations.

faq.question: What creativity levels work best?
faq.answer: Creativity 1-3 produces clean, Swiss-minimal interfaces. Creativity 4-7 gives balanced, personality-forward designs. Creativity 8-10 enables bold, editorial, asymmetric layouts with inline image typography.

faq.question: How does density affect the output?
faq.answer: Low density (1-3) creates gallery-airy layouts with massive whitespace. Medium density (4-7) balances content with breathing room. High density (8-10) produces cockpit-dense, data-heavy interfaces suitable for dashboards.
